finding_results.notes: Compressed the interrogation from five sequential test-fail-correction exchanges to two. Exchange 1 (line 121): the which-hand question, which triggers Maren's confession at line 131. Exchange 2 (lines 137-143): two rapid-fire demands for demonstration (containment pass, residual clear) answered with silence, no answer-key lecturing. At line 145, Thessa glances at her three bowls and silently registers the three grades (active, residual, structural) without quizzing him. The scene then pivots directly through her clean-hands assessment (line 147) and Maren's floor reflection (line 149) to the declaration 'You're not a sin-eater' (line 151), after which the pressure shifts entirely to named local damage (Dunmore/Maret at line 191, Cress/Holloway at line 203, Fenhold/farmer at line 207). Cut material: the bowl-identification test, the kneeling/site-assessment test, the grades quiz, and the smell quiz. The three contamination grade names are preserved through Thessa's table glance at line 145 and the walking-away callback at line 303. Also adjusted the walking-away reflection (line 303) to reference the bowls on Thessa's table rather than holding a practice bowl, since the bowl-handling test was removed.

summary: Compressed the Thessa interrogation test-ladder from five repeated test-fail-correction cycles to two exchanges (the which-hand question plus rapid-fire demonstration demands), then pivoted to named damage as a different pressure source. The revised scene has exactly two sequential test-fail beats before shifting leverage, and each remaining beat does distinct work: the which-hand exchange triggers Maren's confession, the rapid-fire demands confirm total incompetence without lecturing, and the declaration flows directly into the named-damage section where Thessa's anger shifts from procedural testing to specific, countable human cost. Thessa no longer reads as a procedural lecturer; after two probes she stops testing and starts counting.
